Action recognition aims to identify relevant actions described in videos.

Common practices of action recognition involve sampling frames or clips from a given video.

Existing RL-based methods may face training stability issues due to reliance on a large amount of training samples.
RL methods operate under the assumption that frames are selected sequentially depending on observations of previous selections.
The Search-Map-Search (SMS) learning paradigm involves directly searching for the best combination of frames as one entity.
SMS formulates the problem of frame selection through heuristic search in a large space of video frame combinations.
SMS implements a learnable mapping function to generalize to new videos and enhance inference efficiency.
